---
title: "Getting Started"
---

## Prerequisites

Before using this framework, ensure you have the following installed:

- **R** (>= 4.5): [https://cran.r-project.org/](https://cran.r-project.org/)
- **Quarto** (>= 1.6): [https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/)
- **Git**: [https://git-scm.com/](https://git-scm.com/)
- **RStudio** or **VS Code**: Your preferred IDE
- **Node.js** (LTS, optional): For the web application extras

## Quick Start

### 1. Fork and Clone

Fork both repositories on GitHub, then clone them:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/dissertation.git dissertation-rpkg
git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/dissertation-ai.git dissertation-ai
```

### 2. Install the R Package

```r
# From the dissertation-rpkg directory
devtools::install("dissertation")

# Load and verify
library(dissertation)
data(assessmentData)
head(assessmentData)
```

### 3. Explore the Analysis Functions

```r
# Summary statistics
summarizeAssessment()
summarizeAssessment(content_area = "READING", group_by = "GRADE")

# Detailed analyses
analyzeAssessment("achievement_trends")
analyzeAssessment("district_comparison")
analyzeAssessment("score_distribution", grade = "5")

# Chapter information
getChapterSummary("results", include_data = TRUE)

# Discovery
getAvailableAnalyses()
```

### 4. Start the REST API

```r
run_dissertation_api(port = 8000)

# Then visit:
# http://localhost:8000/summary?content_area=READING
# http://localhost:8000/analysis?type=achievement_trends
# http://localhost:8000/openapi.json
```

### 5. Render the Outputs

The same `.qmd` source files produce three different outputs:

```bash
cd dissertation-rpkg/dissertation/quarto_website

# Interactive website (default)
quarto render

# Formal thesis PDF using institutional thesis.cls
quarto render --profile thesis

# Working paper (HTML + PDF) for sharing and versioning
quarto render --profile working-paper
```

## Next Steps

- Read the [Customization Guide](customization_guide.qmd) to adapt the framework for your dissertation
- Explore the [API Reference](api_reference.qmd) for endpoint documentation
- Review [AI Ethics in Research](ai_ethics.qmd) for responsible AI integration
